mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate above 6.5% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, which compresses equity risk appetite and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when UNRATE > 6.5% are not significantly negative compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously to activate.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite joint RSAFS and HOUST contraction, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 105.0 (index 2017=100) signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains, supporting corporate earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns when INDPRO > 105.0 are not significantly positive compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and decline in unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals strong labor market momentum and rising consumer purchasing power, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both conditions to hold simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 150k month-over-month AND UNRATE declines > 0.2pp month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite strong labor market signals, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL above 240.0 (index 1982-1984=100) signals persistent price pressures that force the Federal Reserve to maintain or raise rates, constraining equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: high inflation regime requiring monetary tightening
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when CPIAUCSL > 240.0 are not significantly negative compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured at quarterly frequency above 16000 billion chained 2017 dollars signals sustained economic expansion and corporate profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when GDPC1 > 16000.0 are not significantly positive compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) below 1000 thousand units signals housing market weakness and reduced forward demand for construction materials and consumer durables, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days in regimes where housing is a leading demand indicator.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when HOUST < 1000.0 are not significantly negative compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
